result.claim: We manually collected 1282 raw samples from 126 unique scientific articles.
result.original_text: We manually collected 1282 raw samples from 126 unique scientific articles (Table S1, Supporting Information).

result.claim: The final dataset contains more than 980 nanosized metal, bimetal, and metal oxide materials.
result.original_text: After minimal data cleansing, we obtained the dataset of more than 980 nanosized metals, bimetals, and metal oxides.

result.claim: The data were split into training and test sets in an 80/20 ratio for each regression problem.
result.original_text: Data were normalized and log‑scaled before splitting to train and test sets in the 80/20 ratio.

result.claim: Tenfold cross‑validation was used for model evaluation.
Tree‑based algorithms such as Random Forest, Extra Trees, LGBMRegressor, and XGBRegressor were employed.
result.original_text: Random Forest and Extra Trees regression models ... LGBMRegressor and XGBRegressor algorithms build sequences of decision trees.
result.value: Random Forest, Extra Trees, LGBMRegressor, XGBRegressor

result.claim: Hyperparameter tuning was performed using the Optuna framework.
result.original_text: hyperparameter tuning was done using the model‑agnostic Optuna framework.

result.claim: Descriptors with more than 50% missing values were excluded.
result.original_text: we excluded descriptors with >50% missing values.

result.claim: 211 samples with more than 15% missing values were removed.
result.original_text: We removed the total of 211 samples such that each sample in the remaining dataset had no more than 15% of missing values.

result.claim: k‑NN imputation was used to fill missing values for remaining parameters.
result.original_text: we chose kNN imputation technique, because it considers the relationships between the different features, it is robust to outliers, and it can handle both categorical and continuous variables.

result.claim: Eleven descriptors were used for SAR prediction and eight descriptors for relaxivity prediction.
result.original_text: For prediction of SAR value, 11 descriptors were used, whereas relaxivity values were predicted using 8 descriptors only.

result.claim: The SAR prediction model achieved R² = 0.86 on test data.
The r1 relaxivity model achieved R² = 0.78 on test data.
The r2 relaxivity model achieved R² = 0.75 on test data.
RMSE for SAR prediction on tenfold cross‑validation was 0.28.
RMSE for r1 relaxivity prediction on tenfold cross‑validation was 0.30.
result.original_text: RMSE dropped from 0.30 to 0.27 for r1 relaxivity.

result.claim: RMSE for r2 relaxivity prediction on tenfold cross‑validation was 0.25.
result.original_text: RMSE dropped from 0.25 to 0.22 for r2 relaxivity.

result.claim: A web service called DiMag is available at http://dimag.acidlab.space.
result.original_text: we developed an open access web service, called DiMag (Figure 5), available at http://dimag.acidlab.space.

result.claim: The dataset and code are publicly hosted in a GitHub repository at https://github.com/acid-design-lab/DiMag.
result.original_text: The data that support the findings of this study are openly available in a GitHub repository at https://github.com/acid-design-lab/DiMag.
